# Anti-Patterns — What Keeps Change from Lasting

**Anti-Pattern 1: Looking for a Quick Fix. Real change takes time.**

**Anti-Pattern 2: Waiting for Motivation. Motivation follows action, not the other way.**

**Case: The Expert Hunt. Jumping from guru to guru without implementing anything.**

**Case: The All-or-Nothing. One slip and you give up entirely.**

**Case: The Excuse. Finding reasons why now is not the right time.**

**Case: The Comparison. Measuring your progress against others.**
---

**Case: The Guru. Following influencers without questioning them is a form of relying on experts.**
**Case: The Perfect Plan. Waiting for the perfect plan before starting guarantees you never start.**
**Case: The Approval. Seeking approval before making a change keeps you dependent.**
**Case: The Busy Trap. Staying busy to avoid change is a powerful anti-pattern.**
**Case: The Blame. Blaming circumstances keeps you powerless. Taking responsibility empowers you.**
**Case: The New Year Resolution. Grand resolutions fail. Small sustainable changes last.**
**Case: The All-in. Going all-in on a new habit often leads to burnout and relapse.**
**Case: The Fear. Fear of what others think keeps many people stuck.**
**Case: The Comfort Zone. Staying in the comfort zone is the most comfortable way to stay stuck.**
